project_metadata.lockfile_format_note: bun.lockb is a binary MessagePack-based format used by Bun <1.1. This probe ships a PLACEHOLDER binary, not a real Bun-generated lockfile. A real bun.lockb cannot be generated without running Bun CLI 1.0.x.

mend_expected_behavior.documented_limitation: Mend is NOT a UA-supported resolver for Bun. There is no documented bun.lockb (binary) parser in the UA JavaScript resolver. Detection failure is the expected worst-case.

failure_modes.label: Empty tree + parse error in logs
failure_modes.description: Mend's npm-resolver fallback attempts to read bun.lockb as JSON/JSONC, fails immediately on the binary magic bytes, logs a parse error, and emits an empty dependency tree.

failure_modes.label: Empty tree + silent skip
failure_modes.description: Mend detects bun.lockb is not a JSON file, silently skips it without logging an error, and emits an empty dependency tree. Worse than FM-1 because there is no diagnostic signal.

failure_modes.label: Phantom packages from binary header mis-parse
failure_modes.description: Mend's parser misinterprets binary bytes as partial JSON tokens, producing phantom package names or corrupted version strings. This is the worst case — bad data in output rather than empty output.

failure_modes.label: Positive surprise: binary bun.lockb successfully parsed
failure_modes.description: If Mend has a binary bun.lockb parser (undocumented or added since the last resolver-knowledge sync), the output tree should match probe #10 (bun-1.1-text-lock-probe) for the same package.json. Both hono@^4.0.0 and elysia@^1.0.0 plus their transitives should appear.
failure_modes.expected_tree: non-empty — must match sibling probe #10 tree

warnings: LOCKFILE IS A PLACEHOLDER: The bun.lockb in this probe is a 100-byte binary stub (magic bytes BUN! followed by non-UTF-8 binary content). It is NOT a real Bun-generated lockfile. A real bun.lockb requires running Bun CLI 1.0.x, which cannot be invoked from the probe generator. The placeholder is sufficient to test Mend's response to a binary bun.lockb presence.

EXPECTED EMPTY TREE: Because bun.lockb is a binary format that Mend's documented parsers cannot read, the expected dependency tree is EMPTY (packages: {}, root.dependencies: []). This encodes the documented worst-case (FM-1 / FM-2). If Mend emits a non-empty tree, it is a positive surprise (FM-4) — document and escalate to verify parser correctness against a real bun.lockb.

PAIR V1 — PROBE A: This is the Bun 1.0 side of version-variation pair V1. Same package.json as sibling probe #10 (bun-1.1-text-lock-probe). Only the lockfile format differs: this probe has binary bun.lockb; the sibling has text JSONC bun.lock. If the sibling probe (#10) yields a non-empty tree and this probe (#9) yields an empty tree, the Mend parser handles Bun 1.1+ text format only and silently fails on Bun 1.0 binary format.

Paired probe: bun-1.1-text-lock-probe (#10) at https://github.com/mend-detection-qa/bun-1.1-text-lock-probe. Same package.json (name=bun-version-pair-probe, hono ^4.0.0, elysia ^1.0.0); only the lockfile format differs.

Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool supported list. scanSettings.versioning cannot pin Bun toolchain. No .whitesource file is emitted for this probe. Detection MUST be lockfile-driven and entirely static.

PROBE IS EXPLORATORY: Bun binary lockfile parsing is not documented in the UA JavaScript resolver knowledge. If Mend emits any result (empty or non-empty), capture the raw scan output and compare against the sibling probe (#10) result. This is one half of the V1 paired regression surface.
